# Google Calendar Connector - Full Documentation

# Google Calendar Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Google Calendar (Google Calendar Connector) enables you to interface with the
Google Calendar service without having to interact with the Google Calendar API directly.

## Configurations

---

### Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_Oauth2">Oauth2 Connection Provider</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Oauth2 Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection will be closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of outbound connections to keep open at the same time</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not received responses should be streamed</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response will be stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether your application can refresh access tokens when the user is not using the browser to access the application. Valid values are <code>online</code> and <code>offline</code>. If <code>offline</code> is set, a refresh token is retrieved.</p></td><td><p><code>offline</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to retrieve an access token or not. Valid values are <code>consent</code>, <code>none</code>, or <code>select_account</code>. Must be set to <code>consent</code> to retrieve a refresh token.</p></td><td><p><code>consent</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Uri</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance/tenant gets its own</p></td><td><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are HTTP and HTTPS</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumerKey as registered with the service provider</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumerSecret as registered with the service provider</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs authorization endpoint URL</p></td><td><p><code>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs accessToken endpoint URL</p></td><td><p><code>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth scopes to be requested during the dance. If not provided, this value defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td><td><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar</code> <code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.events</code> <code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.events.readonly</code> <code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly</code> <code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.settings.readonly</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The resourceOwnerId which each component should use if it doesnât reference otherwise.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a flow to execute right before starting the OAuth dance</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a flow to execute right after an accessToken has been received</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to an &lt;http:listener-config /&gt; to use to create the listener that will catch the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path of the access token callback endpoint</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path of the local HTTP endpoint which triggers the OAuth dance</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the callback endpoint is behind a proxy or should be accessed through a non direct URL, use this parameter to tell the OAuth provider the URL it should use to access the callback</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store that should be used to store each resource owner idâs data. If not specified, runtime will automatically provision the default one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Calendars insert](#CreateCalendars)
- [Acl insert](#CreateCalendarsAclByCalendarId)
- [Acl watch](#CreateCalendarsAclWatchByCalendarId)
- [Calendars clear](#CreateCalendarsClearByCalendarId)
- [Events insert](#CreateCalendarsEventsByCalendarId)
- [Events import](#CreateCalendarsEventsImportByCalendarId)
- [Events move](#CreateCalendarsEventsMoveByCalendarIdEventId)
- [Events quick Add](#CreateCalendarsEventsQuickAddByCalendarId)
- [Events watch](#CreateCalendarsEventsWatchByCalendarId)
- [Channels stop](#CreateChannelsStop)
- [Freebusy query](#CreateFreeBusy)
- [Calendar List insert](#CreateUsersMeCalendarList)
- [Calendar List watch](#CreateUsersMeCalendarListWatch)
- [Settings watch](#CreateUsersMeSettingsWatch)
- [Acl delete](#DeleteCalendarsAclByCalendarIdRuleId)
- [Calendars delete](#DeleteCalendarsByCalendarId)
- [Events delete](#DeleteCalendarsEventsByCalendarIdEventId)
- [Calendar List delete](#DeleteUsersMeCalendarListByCalendarId)
- [Acl list](#GetCalendarsAclByCalendarId)
- [Acl get](#GetCalendarsAclByCalendarIdRuleId)
- [Calendars get](#GetCalendarsByCalendarId)
- [Events list](#GetCalendarsEventsByCalendarId)
- [Events get](#GetCalendarsEventsByCalendarIdEventId)
- [Events instances](#GetCalendarsEventsInstancesByCalendarIdEventId)
- [Colors get](#GetColors)
- [Calendar List list](#GetUsersMeCalendarList)
- [Calendar List get](#GetUsersMeCalendarListByCalendarId)
- [Settings list](#GetUsersMeSettings)
- [Settings get](#GetUsersMeSettingsBySetting)
- [Acl patch](#PatchCalendarsAclByCalendarIdRuleId)
- [Calendars patch](#PatchCalendarsByCalendarId)
- [Events patch](#PatchCalendarsEventsByCalendarIdEventId)
- [Calendar List patch](#PatchUsersMeCalendarListByCalendarId)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)
- [Acl update](#UpdateCalendarsAclByCalendarIdRuleId)
- [Calendars update](#UpdateCalendarsByCalendarId)
- [Events update](#UpdateCalendarsEventsByCalendarIdEventId)
- [Calendar List update](#UpdateUsersMeCalendarListByCalendarId)

## Associated Sources

- [On Updated Events Trigger](#OnUpdatedEventsTrigger)

## Operations

## Calendars insert

`<google-calendar:create-calendars>`

Creates a secondary calendar. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /calendars endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Request Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Acl insert

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-acl-by-calendar-id>`

Creates an access control rule. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/calendars/{calendarId}/acl endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDS, call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Notifications</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Optional. The default is True.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acl Rule Request Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Acl watch

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-acl-watch-by-calendar-id>`

Watch for changes to ACL resources. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/calendars/{calendarId}/acl/watch endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDS, call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of entries returned on one result page. By default the value is 100 entries. The page size can never be larger than 250 entries. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Deleted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include deleted ACLs in the result. Deleted ACLs are represented by a role equal to "none". Deleted ACLs are always included if the syncToken is provided. Optional.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sync Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of results from the previous list request. It makes the result of this list request contain only entries that have changed since then. All entries deleted since the previous list request will always be in the result set and it is not allowed to set showDeleted to False. If the syncToken expires, the server will respond with a 410 GONE response code and the client should clear its storage and perform a full synchronization without any syncToken. The default is to return all entries.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Request Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Calendars clear

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-clear-by-calendar-id>`

Clears a primary calendar. This operation deletes all events associated with the primary calendar of
an account. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /calendars/{calendarId}/clear endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDS, call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Events insert

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-events-by-calendar-id>`

Creates an event. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /calendars/{calendarId}/events
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDS, call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Updates</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>EXTERNAL_ONLY</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to send notifications about the creation of the new event. Note that some emails might still be sent. The default is false.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Conference Data Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version number of conference data supported by the API client. Version 0 assumes no conference data support and ignores conference data in the eventâs body. Version 1 enables support for copying of ConferenceData as well as for creating new conferences using the createRequest field of conferenceData. The default is 0.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Attachments</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether API client performing operation supports event attachments. Optional. The default is False.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Attendees</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of attendees to include in the response. If there are more than the specified number of attendees, only the participant is returned. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Request Insert Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Config](#Config)

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Events import

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-events-import-by-calendar-id>`

Imports an event. This operation is used to add a private copy of an existing event to a calendar.
This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /calendars/{calendarId}/events/import endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDS, call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Conference Data Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version number of conference data supported by the API client. Version 0 assumes no conference data support and ignores conference data in the eventâs body. Version 1 enables support for copying ConferenceData as well as for creating new conferences using the createRequest field of conferenceData. The default is 0.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Attachments</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether API client performing operation supports event attachments. Optional. The default is False.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Request Import Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND

## Events move

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-events-move-by-calendar-id-event-id>`

Moves an event to another calendar, which changes an eventâs organizer. This operation makes an HTTP
POST request to the /calendars/{calendarId}/events/{eventId}/move endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier of the source calendar where the event currently is on.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Event identifier.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Updates</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>EXTERNAL_ONLY</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Guests who should receive notifications about the change of the eventâs organizer.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>destination</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier of the target calendar where the event is to be moved to.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Events quick Add

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-events-quick-add-by-calendar-id>`

Creates an event based on a simple text string. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/calendars/{calendarId}/events/quickAdd endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDS, call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Updates</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>EXTERNAL_ONLY</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Guests who should receive notifications about the creation of the new event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The text describing the event to be created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Events watch

`<google-calendar:create-calendars-events-watch-by-calendar-id>`

Watch for changes to Events resources. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/calendars/{calendarId}/events/watch endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDS, call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Min</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Lower bound (exclusive) for an eventâs end time to filter by. Optional. The default is not to filter by end time. Must be an RFC3339 timestamp with mandatory time zone offset, for example, 2011-06-03T10:00:00-07:00, 2011-06-03T10:00:00Z. Milliseconds may be provided but are ignored. If timeMax is set, timeMin must be smaller than timeMax.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>iCalUID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the event ID in the iCalendar format to be included in the response. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private Extended Property</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Extended properties constraint specified as propertyName=value. Matches only private properties. This parameter might be repeated multiple times to return events that match all given constraints.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Hidden Invitations</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include hidden invitations in the result. Optional. The default is False.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of events returned on one result page. The number of events in the resulting page may be less than this value, or none at all, even if there are more events matching the query. Incomplete pages can be detected by a non-empty nextPageToken field in the response. By default the value is 250 events. The page size can never be larger than 2500 events. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared Extended Property</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Extended properties constraint specified as propertyName=value. Matches only shared properties. This parameter might be repeated multiple times to return events that match all given constraints.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Min</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Lower bound for an eventâs last modification time (as a RFC3339 timestamp) to filter by. When specified, entries deleted since this time will always be included regardless of showDeleted. Optional. The default is not to filter by last modification time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Deleted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include deleted events (with status equals "cancelled") in the result. Cancelled instances of recurring events (but not the underlying recurring event) will still be included if showDeleted and singleEvents are both False. If showDeleted and singleEvents are both True, only single instances of deleted events (but not the underlying recurring events) are returned. Optional. The default is False.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Attendees</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of attendees to include in the response. If there are more than the specified number of attendees, only the participant is returned. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>q</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Free text search terms to find events that match these terms in any field, except for extended properties. Optional.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Single Events</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to expand recurring events into instances and only return single one-off events and instances of recurring events, but not the underlying recurring events themselves. Optional. The default is False.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order By</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>START_TIME</p></li><li><p>UPDATED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The order of the events returned in the result. Optional. The default is an unspecified, stable order.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sync Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of results from the previous list request. It makes the result of this list request contain only entries that have changed since then. All events deleted since the previous list request will always be in the result set and it is not allowed to set showDeleted to False. The following query parameters cannot be specified together with nextSyncToken to ensure consistency of the client state: iCalUID, orderBy, privateExtendedProperty, q, sharedExtendedProperty, timeMin, timeMax, and updatedMin. If the syncToken expires, the server will respond with a 410 GONE response code and the client should clear its storage and perform a full synchronization without any syncToken. Optional. The default is to return all entries.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Zone</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time zone used in the response. Optional. The default is the time zone of the calendar.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Max</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Upper bound (exclusive) for an eventâs start time to filter by. Optional. The default is not to filter by start time. Must be an RFC3339 timestamp with mandatory time zone offset, for example, 2011-06-03T10:00:00-07:00, 2011-06-03T10:00:00Z. Milliseconds may be provided but are ignored. If timeMin is set, timeMax must be greater than timeMin.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Request Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Channels stop

`<google-calendar:create-channels-stop>`

Stop watching resources through this channel This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/channels/stop endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Request Stop Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Freebusy query

`<google-calendar:create-free-busy>`

Returns free/busy information for a set of calendars. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to
the /freeBusy endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Free Busy Request Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Calendar List insert

`<google-calendar:create-users-me-calendar-list>`

Inserts an existing calendar into the user''s calendar list. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /users/me/calendarList endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. If empty, all fields will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Color Rgb Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use the foregroundColor and backgroundColor fields to write the calendar colors (RGB). If this feature is used, the index-based colorId field will be set to the best matching option automatically. Optional. The default is False.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar List Entry Request Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-CALENDAR:CLIENT_ERROR
